what_it_does: Application-level stylesheet for the Mockup Studio shell. It defines the warm plaster wall background, the app grid, the unified spine-and-drawer navigator, lifecycle/project views, operations widgets, shared components (cards, buttons, chips, Vi badge, connectors, ask bar, orbital assistant), and three switchable navigator join treatments (unfold, tab, bridge).
observations: Inherits ConceptV tokens from colors_and_type.css via CSS custom properties.
Builds a layered 'wall' background with radial gradients, SVG frond texture, and stippled noise.
App grid is 72px rail + canvas; studio mode expands the left region to 328px for spine + drawer.
Navigator is designed as one continuous warm-paper surface rather than separate sidebars.
Active items share a repeated treatment: warm-white background, inset gold ring, and a gold left rib.
Zone navigation redesign uses spine zone symbols and a drawer page list with gold active indicators.
Three navigator styles are toggled with a data-nav-style attribute: unfold, tab, bridge.
Legacy .rail styles are retained but marked as unused.
File is truncated partway through the .vi-panel section.
standouts: The 'one continuous navigator' concept with a connected hinge between spine and drawer.
Three distinct CSS-only navigator join treatments driven by a data attribute.
Warm textured wall background with organic frond SVG overlay.
Unified active-state language across spine buttons, drawer items, and zone pages.
conventions: BEM-like naming with __ elements and -- modifiers.
Heavy reliance on CSS custom properties for color, type, radius, duration, and easing tokens.
Transform-only animations where possible, with reduced-motion fallbacks.
Comments used to explain design intent and spatial metaphors.
Hardcoded fallback values inside var() declarations.
concerns: Large monolithic file mixing layout, navigation architecture, theme treatments, and component styles.
Repetition of active-state styling across spine, drawer, and page selectors.
Truncated content leaves the .vi-panel rules incomplete.
Complex z-index layering may be brittle (wall 0, nav 6, topbar 10, ask-bar 30, orbit 40).
notes: This is the central application stylesheet that turns the ConceptV token system into the warm-paper Mockup Studio shell, organized around the idea of a navigator that is one continuous unfolding surface.
questions: Which components consume the truncated .vi-panel rules?
Are the legacy .rail styles kept purely for fallback or still referenced somewhere?
How is the data-nav-style attribute changed at runtime?
Do the spine zone symbols and drawer pages share a single component, or are they separate?
What is the intended motion behavior of the 'lifted seat' active indicator when switching pages?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is application styles inheriting ConceptV tokens, which matches. The file also goes much further, encoding navigation architecture, spatial metaphors, and component taxonomy.
apparent_themes: Continuity and spatial metaphor in navigation.
Quiet hierarchy through gold accents, soft shadows, and hairline dividers.
One-surface navigation instead of stacked independent sidebars.
intention_signals: The stylesheet is trying to make the Studio feel like a single physical sheet of warm paper that unfolds: the rail, drawer, and zone pages read as one continuous surface linked by a gold hinge, while the wall background and soft shadows evoke a studio wall with frond shadows.
novelty: The three switchable navigator join treatments (unfold/tab/bridge) expressed purely through CSS data attributes, and the unified spine+drawer+zone navigation treated as one warm-paper surface.
contribution: A complete visual language for the application shell: the warm textured canvas, the continuous navigator with its active-state hinge, lifecycle/project view layouts, and reusable component primitives that a fused system could keep as its shared styling substrate.
summary_for_embedding: Application-level CSS for the Mockup Studio shell implementing a warm-paper design system with a unified spine-and-drawer navigator, lifecycle/project views, and shared UI components.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the single stylesheet that realizes the ConceptV token system as the cohesive visual and navigational surface of the Mockup Studio app.
